title: Fix UVM function output width reassignment
body:
Fix UVM function output width reassignment.  This is one of a few remaining blockers to UVM #1538.

This can be seen by editing test_regress/t/t_uvm_all.pl to remove --debug-exit-uvm, and commenting out the line to workaround #4466.


```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t/t_uvm_pkg_all.vh:13459:76: Unsupported: Function output argument 'value' requires 4096 bits, but connection's VARREF 'recording_detail' generates 32 bits.
13459 |   if (!uvm_config_db #(uvm_bitstream_t)::get(this, "", "recording_detail", recording_detail))
      |                                                                            ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t/t_uvm_pkg_all.vh:15865:75: Unsupported: Function output argument 'value' requires 4096 bits, but connection's VARREF 'tmp' generates 32 bits.
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t/t_uvm_pkg_all.vh:26283:57: Unsupported: Function output argument 'value' requires 1024 bits, but connection's VARREF 'val' generates 64 bits.
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t/t_uvm_pkg_all.vh:26289:58: Unsupported: Function output argument 'value' requires 1024 bits, but connection's VARREF 'slice' generates 64 bits.
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t/t_uvm_pkg_all.vh:29553:44: Unsupported: Function output argument 'value' requires 1024 bits, but connection's VARREF 'slice' generates 64 bits.
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t/t_uvm_pkg_all.vh:34167:38: Unsupported: Function output argument 'value' requires 1024 bits, but connection's VARREF 'd' generates 64 bits.
```
